results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

results.contract_source_text: Data relating to criminal convictions and offences (cf. GDPR art. 10). *Sensitive data includes: data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious and/or philosophical beliefs, trade union membership, processing of genetic data or biometric data for the purpose of uniquely identifying a natural person, data concerning health and/or data concerning a natural person's sex life or sexual orientation Please note that for the processing of sensitive data, explicit consent of the Data Subject is required. Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

results.contract_source_text: Data relating to criminal convictions and offences (cf. GDPR art. 10). *Sensitive data includes: data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious and/or philosophical beliefs, trade union membership, processing of genetic data or biometric data for the purpose of uniquely identifying a natural person, data concerning health and/or data concerning a natural person's sex life or sexual orientation Please note that for the processing of sensitive data, explicit consent of the Data Subject is required. Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

results.contract_source_text: If the Controller requests the Processor to document its Sub-Processor’s compliance with the obligations laid down in Article 28 of the GDPR the Processor shall be entitled to fulfil this obligation by referring Controller to the relevant audit reports or similar documentation made available by the respective Sub-Processors with information indicating that the Sub-Processor in question complies with the GDPR, provided that such audit report(s) or similar documentation shall be based on applicable, acknowledged audit standards, e.g. ISAE 3000 or 3402, ISO 27001 or similar standards. results.core_alignment_check: Yes: both concern the processor's security-related obligation for personal data processing. The regulation specifically requires the processor, when assessing security level, to take into account risks of accidental/unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorized disclosure, or access. The contract requires the processor to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures so that processing meets GDPR requirements and protects data subjects' rights, which substantively covers security measures and risk-aware protection.
results.condition_check: Compatible: the regulation applies when assessing the level of security; the contract states the measures must be implemented in a manner that ensures GDPR compliance and protection of rights, which is not narrower and is consistent with the security assessment context.
results.modality_check: Compatible: 'agrees to' in this structured obligation functions as a binding contractual commitment to implement measures, which is at least as strong as the regulatory 'shall'.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract's manner requirement is broader/stricter, requiring measures to ensure GDPR compliance and protection of rights; it does not contradict or weaken the regulation's security-risk consideration.
results.contract_source_text: The Processor agrees to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures in such a manner that the Processing of the Personal Data will meet the requirements of the GDPR and other applicable national and EU data protection law and ensure the protection of the rights of the Data Subjects.

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation gives the controller a right to terminate the DPA in certain cases; the contract gives each party an entitlement to terminate the agreement if the parties cannot agree in good faith on reasonable terms for instruction changes needed for GDPR/legal compliance. This is the same general termination right, but the contract is broader in actor and narrower/specific in trigger, and it is framed as termination of the agreement rather than expressly the DPA.
results.condition_check: The contract condition is substantially compatible with the regulation's 'in certain cases' because it is tied to necessary GDPR or legal compliance changes and failure to agree on reasonable terms.
results.modality_check: The contract uses 'shall be entitled to terminate,' which is at least as strong as a right to terminate.
results.other_constraints_check: The 60-day written notice and good-faith negotiation are additional procedural constraints, but they do not make the right looser than the regulation; they are compatible as stricter procedural detail.
results.contract_source_text: The Processor shall use reasonable endeavours to comply with any legislative changes. However, the Processor shall not be obligated to implement any change of the instructions if the Parties cannot in good faith agree to reasonable terms for the implementation. If the Parties fail to agree in good faith to reasonable terms regarding change of the instructions each Party shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement with a written notice of 60 days, provided that such changes are deemed necessary to comply with the GDPR or other applicable EU or national data protection laws and regulation. The Main Agreement and any other agreement between the Parties involving Processing of Personal Data shall automatically terminate at the same time.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object: the regulation requires the processor to take all Article 32 security measures / ensure processing security; the contract parent/sub-item addresses security measures, but the sub-item merely points to measures being specified in an external website/policies rather than itself imposing the measures, and the parent only covers one testing/evaluating measure, not all required Article 32 measures
results.condition_check: No condition mismatch; both are unconditional.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a plain statement, but the substantive issue is not modality; it does not itself impose the full mandatory security obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: No timing/manner/location issue; however the clause is a pointer to external policies and the parent is narrower than the regulation's full Article 32 obligation.
results.contract_source_text: a process for testing, assessing and evaluating the effectiveness of technical and organisational measures for ensuring the security of the Processing. 2. In assessing the appropriate level of security account shall be taken in particular of the risks that are presented by Processing, in particular from accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorised disclosure of, or access to Personal Data transmitted, stored or otherwise processed. 3. This is the same security/protection obligation, and the contract is broader/stricter.
results.condition_check: No condition on either side; compatible.
results.modality_check: Regulation uses 'can' (permission/ability framing) for including the measure, while the contract imposes an affirmative obligation ('agrees to implement'). This is equal-or-greater force.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract's manner clause is at least as protective and broader than the regulation's security requirement; no conflicting timing, target, location, or cause constraints.
results.contract_source_text: The Processor agrees to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures in such a manner that the Processing of the Personal Data will meet the requirements of the GDPR and other applicable national and EU data protection law and ensure the protection of the rights of the Data Subjects.

results.other_constraints_check: Timing and manner differ but are not the main issue; the contract is about immediate written notice to the processor, not assistance in communicating a breach to data subjects.

results.contract_source_text: If the Controller requests the Processor to document its Sub-Processor’s compliance with the obligations laid down in Article 28 of the GDPR the Processor shall be entitled to fulfil this obligation by referring Controller to the relevant audit reports or similar documentation made available by the respective Sub-Processors with information indicating that the Sub-Processor in question complies with the GDPR, provided that such audit report(s) or similar documentation shall be based on applicable, acknowledged audit standards, e.g. ISAE 3000 or 3402, ISO 27001 or similar standards. Any additional audit reporting or additional other similar documentation requested by the Controller will be at the Controller’s cost and expenses and is charged under the conditions set out in Appendix 3. 11. results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation requires the processor to remain fully liable to the controller for the performance of sub-processor obligations. The contract clause is a liability carve-out stating that the processor’s liability limitations do not apply to losses/costs suffered by the controller arising from the processor’s own failure to comply with supervisory-authority obligations or supervisory-authority penalties due to the processor’s material breach. This addresses processor liability toward the controller, but it is not the same obligation as remaining fully liable for sub-processor performance.
results.condition_check: Compatible in the sense that both concern liability in breach-related situations, but the contract condition is narrower and different: it is tied to supervisory-authority compliance failures and penalties, not sub-processor obligations.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a restrictive 'shall not apply' formulation, which is strong enough in itself, but it applies to a different liability scenario.
results.other_constraints_check: No timing, manner, target, location, or cause mismatch relevant beyond the substantive mismatch in object and trigger.
results.contract_source_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, the limitations of the Processor’s liability set out in this clause 13 shall not apply to loss suffered or costs incurred by the Controller due to the Processor’s failure to comply with its obligations towards a supervisory authority or penalties imposed by a supervisory authority due to the Processor’s material breach.

The Processor may – unless otherwise specifically is set out in the Agreement – apply all relevant means, including software, servers and IT systems.

Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

The contract parent/sub-item instead limits liability to direct damages, caps it at three months after a claim, and carves out willful misconduct or gross negligence. That is a different and narrower liability regime, not the same obligation.
results.condition_check: Not aligned. The regulation condition is breach of processor-specific GDPR duties or acting outside/contrary to lawful controller instructions. The contract condition is a liability cap / insurance-related limitation and a misconduct carve-out, which does not match the regulatory trigger.
results.modality_check: The contract is a plain statement within a mandatory parent, but it is not an obligation to be liable in the regulatory sense; it is a limitation on liability. So modality does not rescue the mismatch.
results.other_constraints_check: Timing and scope are materially different: 'months after a claim was made' and limitation to direct damages are additional restrictions not present in the regulation and make the clause looser on the required liability obligation.
results.contract_source_text: months after a claim was made, unless the damage has resulted from a willful misconduct or gross negligence on the Processor's part.

results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the regulation requires the processor to process personal data only if required by Union or Member State law and without documented instructions, while the contract authorises processing on the terms of the agreement and says the agreement constitutes the instructions; this is a different obligation and does not address the legal-law trigger or the absence of documented instructions.
results.condition_check: Mismatch: the regulation is conditional on being required by Union or Member State law; the contract has no such condition and instead ties processing to the agreement's terms/instructions.
results.modality_check: The contract is a permission/authorisation tied to instructions, not an obligation matching the regulatory conditional permission to process without documented instructions.
results.other_constraints_check: Mismatch: the regulation requires processing without documented instructions; the contract expressly says the agreement constitutes the instructions and processing is on the terms and conditions set out in the agreement.
results.contract_source_text: The Controller hereby authorises the Processor to Process the Personal Data on behalf of the Controller on the terms and conditions set out in this Agreement. The Processor shall Process the Personal Data only on documented instructions from the Controller. The Parties agree that this Agreement shall constitute the instructions as of the date of the Agreement. The Processor may – unless otherwise specifically is set out in the Agreement – apply all relevant means, including software, servers and IT systems.

results.other_constraints_check: No timing/manner/target/location/cause issue; however the substantive content required by the regulation (consequences of the breach) is missing.

results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

results.modality_check: regulation is a prohibition ('shall not'); contract is an affirmative authorization, which is not looser on its face but addresses a different transfer context
results.other_constraints_check: recipient/location differ materially: third country or international organization versus third party provider of an integration service/application
results.contract_source_text: As part of the Main Agreement the Controller may also choose to use third party integration services and/or applications made available by the Processor on Processor.com in cooperation with the third party providers of such integration services and applications. If the Controller uses such third party integration services and/or applications it hereby authorises the Processor to: i) provide, transmit or transfer the data, including Personal Data, of the Controller to the third party provider of the relevant integration service and/or application, provided and only to the extent this is necessary for the performance and use by Controller of the said integration services and/or applications, and ii) Process data, including Personal Data, of the Controller that are transferred from the third party provider of the relevant integration service and/or application to the Controller’s Services with Processor.

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the controller to have a right to terminate the DPA in certain cases. The contract sub-item, read with the parent context, concerns the processor giving the controller access to system functionality so the controller can delete and/or return/export its data. That is a data-return/deletion assistance obligation, not a termination right or termination of the DPA itself.
results.condition_check: No matching termination condition is provided; the contract clause is about deletion/export access, not termination in certain cases.
results.modality_check: The contract uses 'will' for access/deletion assistance, but this does not impose the controller's right to terminate the DPA.
results.other_constraints_check: The timing/manner language about exporting/deleting data is unrelated to the regulation's termination-right obligation.
results.contract_source_text: Processor will give Controller access to system functionality in order for the Controller to delete and/or return (i.e. export) any and all of Controller’s data including the Personal Data during the Agreement. Upon expiry of the Agreement the Processor will delete all of Controller’s data including the Personal Data, unless EU or national Member State law requires storage of the Personal Data.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align: the regulation requires the processor to take into account the risk to personal data when assessing security; the contract parent requires implementing appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure security appropriate to risk, and the sub-item adds testing/assessing/evaluating effectiveness of those measures. This is related to security assessment, but it does not expressly require taking into account the specific risk of accidental/unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorized disclosure or access to personal data.
results.condition_check: Compatible in part: both are triggered in the security-assessment context, but the contract condition is broader (general security/risk context) rather than the specific assessment of security level tied to the listed personal-data risks.
results.modality_check: Mandatory enough: the parent clause is mandatory and the sub-item is part of that obligation; the plain statement sub-item is treated as mandatory within the parent context.
results.other_constraints_check: No direct mismatch on timing, manner, target, location, or cause; however the contract omits the specific risk factors and focuses on effectiveness testing of measures rather than explicitly taking those risks into account.

results.contract_source_text: The Processor shall use reasonable endeavours to comply with any legislative changes. However, the Processor shall not be obligated to implement any change of the instructions if the Parties cannot in good faith agree to reasonable terms for the implementation. If the Parties fail to agree in good faith to reasonable terms regarding change of the instructions each Party shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement with a written notice of 60 days, provided that such changes are deemed necessary to comply with the GDPR or other applicable EU or national data protection laws and regulation. The Main Agreement and any other agreement between the Parties involving Processing of Personal Data shall automatically terminate at the same time.

results.other_constraints_check: The contract's one-year timing is unrelated to the regulation's liability scope and is materially different, not equivalent or stricter.

Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to assist the controller in ensuring security of processing. results.condition_check: The regulation has no condition. The contract is triggered upon expiry of the Agreement, which is unrelated to the security-assistance duty.
results.modality_check: The regulation uses shall, while the contract uses will; however modality is not the main issue because the contract addresses a different duty.
results.other_constraints_check: No relevant overlap on manner, timing, target, location, or cause. The contract's expiry-based deletion/return context does not match security assistance.

If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

The Parties agree that this Agreement shall constitute the instructions as of the date of the Agreement. The Processor may – unless otherwise specifically is set out in the Agreement – apply all relevant means, including software, servers and IT systems.

results.contract_source_text: Data relating to criminal convictions and offences (cf. GDPR art. 10). *Sensitive data includes: data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious and/or philosophical beliefs, trade union membership, processing of genetic data or biometric data for the purpose of uniquely identifying a natural person, data concerning health and/or data concerning a natural person's sex life or sexual orientation Please note that for the processing of sensitive data, explicit consent of the Data Subject is required. Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient do not match: regulation requires the processor to inform the controller of a legal requirement before processing, while the contract gives the controller a permissive right to amend/specify instructions
results.condition_check: Not compatible: regulation is triggered only if law does not prohibit informing the controller on public-interest grounds; contract applies at any time
results.modality_check: Weaker: regulation imposes a mandatory duty ('shall'); contract is permissive ('may')
results.other_constraints_check: Not aligned: timing differs ('before processing' vs no timing duty), and the contract's manner reference to clause 11 concerns instruction amendment, not informing about a legal requirement
results.contract_source_text: The Controller may at any time amend or specify the instructions in accordance with clause 11 of this Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, clause 11 can only be amended according to mutual agreement between the Parties.

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires including/ensuring confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of processing systems and services. The contract parent clause requires appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, and the sub-item specifically requires encryption of personal data in transit and for remote access. Encryption is a concrete security measure that contributes to confidentiality and overall security, but it does not itself cover the full obligation to ensure confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of processing systems and services.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are conditioned on taking account of state of the art, costs, nature/scope/context/purposes, and risk.
results.modality_check: Compatible and stronger: contract uses 'shall' and a mandatory parent clause, which is at least as strong as 'can' in the regulation.
results.other_constraints_check: No mismatch on timing or target; however the contract is narrower in substance because it specifies only encryption rather than the broader security properties required.

The Main Agreement and any other agreement between the Parties involving Processing of Personal Data shall automatically terminate at the same time.
